statement.en: Dataset about substitution of Mg2+ cofactor with Ca2+ disrupt positive cooperativity in F1FO-ATP(hydrol)ase catalysis
statement.summary: This dataset contains data from the evaluation of the hydrolytic activity of mitochondrial F1FO-ATPase, isolated from porcine heart, activated by the natural cofactor Mg²⁺ or replaced by Ca²⁺. The aim of the research was to investigate how replacing the physiological cofactor Mg²⁺ with Ca²⁺ affects the catalysis, cooperativity, and mechanochemical functionality of mitochondrial F1FO-ATPase, in order to understand the molecular mechanisms that determine the transition of the enzyme from its physiological role as an ATP synthase (the "enzyme of life") to its pathological function as a component of the mitochondrial permeability transition pore (mPTP, "enzyme of death"). "F_ATPase_cooperativity_Dataset.zip" contains data on the activity of the Mg2+-dependent F1FO-ATPase, which is more efficie

proposer: Cristina, Algieri, Antonia, Cugliari, Fabiana, Trombetti et al.

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: Dataset represents empirical experimental observations (2026 date suggests preprint/provisional status). Cooperativity claims are testable but require peer validation. The mechanochemical mechanism underlying Mg²⁺/Ca²⁺ substitution effects is partially established biochemistry, but the specific dataset's novel findings remain unvalidated.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: Mg²⁺の協調性は分母の抑制を通じて触媒効率を増幅し、Ca²⁺はこの対称性を破壊する。

visualGlyph.interpretation: 三つの結合サイトが調和した青緑色の合唱から、破線のピンクの不協和へと崩壊する。
